action: Open the Start menu by clicking the Windows icon at the bottom left of the screen. Then type 'Photoshop' in the Start menu search bar and select Adobe Photoshop from the search results to open it.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: is the first action. The task is to open the Start menu by clicking the Windows icon at the bottom left of the screen. I can see the Windows icon (Box ID 113) in the bottom left corner. I will click it to open the Start menu.

previous action: I clicked the Windows icon at the bottom left to open the Start menu. The expected outcome is that the Start menu should appear. Observing the current screen, the lower left now shows the Start menu with a search bar and various app icons, confirming the menu is open. The next step is to type 'Photoshop' in the Start menu search bar to search for Adobe Photoshop.

previous action: I typed 'Photoshop' in the Start menu search bar. The expected outcome is that the search results should update to show Adobe Photoshop as an option. Observing the current screen, I can see 'Adobe Photoshop Elements' (Box ID 201) listed among the app icons, which matches the search. The next step is to select Adobe Photoshop from the search results to open it. I will click on the 'Adobe Photoshop Elements' icon (Box ID 201) to launch the application.

previous action: I was instructed to click on the 'Adobe Photoshop Elements' icon (Box ID 201) to launch the application. The expected outcome is that Adobe Photoshop Elements should begin launching, which may be indicated by a splash screen, loading animation, or the application window appearing. Observing the current screen, I see that the Start menu is no longer in focus and the desktop is visible, but I do not yet see a Photoshop Elements window or splash screen.
